page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.medium is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing a direct physical-to-virtual core allocation. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 4.0 GB of RAM per core.

This server type does not include local storage or integrated GPU hardware, requiring external network-attached storage for persistent data. The underlying CPU lacks support for advanced instruction sets such as Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX, AVX512), Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX), and hardware-accelerated cryptography (AES, SHA_NI), which limits its suitability for vector-heavy mathematical computations or hardware-accelerated encryption tasks.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the dedicated CPU allocation provides predictable compute performance without the resource contention typical of shared-core or burstable instances. Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the instance is structured for general-purpose workloads. It is suitable for standard web serving, application hosting, and moderate database operations that do not require specialized hardware acceleration or high-performance local storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.m1.medium is an x86_64 general-purpose virtual machine instance operating on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores, providing a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio and eliminating hyper-threading. The instance features 16.0 GB of system memory, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying entirely on network-attached storage. The CPU architecture lacks advanced instruction sets like AVX, AVX512, AMX, and hardware-accelerated cryptography. This resource profile makes the instance qualitatively cost-effective for workloads requiring dedicated, predictable CPU performance without the need for specialized vector processing or local disk I/O. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and lightweight database hosting.

tagline: A dedicated x86_64 virtual server with a balanced resource profile for predictable general-purpose compute workloads.
